news.headline: Iranian missiles over Tel Aviv prompt sirens, interceptor launches
news.summary: Witness videos captured Iranian projectiles soaring over Tel Aviv as sirens blared and Israeli interceptors launched.

news.headline: Iran’s legal case for striking the Gulf collapses under scrutiny
news.summary: Claims of self-defence cannot justify missile attacks on neighbouring states that were not party to the conflict.

news.headline: Wake up before it’s too late: Oppose the unjust, cruel war on Iran
news.summary: Silence in the face of US–Israeli aggression will destroy the international legal order and endanger global peace.

news.headline: ‘We stand with Israel’: Germany’s Friedrich Merz scolds protester
news.summary: “We stand with Israel.” German Chancellor Friedrich Merz scolded a pro-Palestinian protester at an election rally.

news.headline: Iran’s strikes on the Gulf: Burning the bridges of good neighbourliness
news.summary: The Gulf states didn't want this war, and they're the ones Iran needs to mediate an end to the conflict.

news.headline: Iran war is latest threat to a global economy rattled by Trump
news.summary: Rising energy prices threaten to stoke inflation and hobble economic growth in countries around the world.

news.headline: Cuba announces fifth death after shootout with Florida-tagged speedboat
news.summary: The government in Havana has claimed that the 10 people on board speedboat planned to 'unleash terrorism' in Cuba.

news.headline: Iran war live: Trump says no deal with Iran until ‘unconditional surrender’
news.summary: US-Israeli attacks on Iran continue as Israel's bombing of Lebanon kills at least 217 people, injures almost 800.

news.headline: Cuba closes Quito embassy after Ecuador’s Daniel Noboa expels its diplomats
news.summary: Noboa's government gave Cuba's diplomats 48 hours to leave Ecuador, though it did not provide an explanation.

news.headline: Drone evades defences, ignites fire at southern Iraq oil facility
news.summary: A drone evaded defences and ignited a massive fire at an oil facility for foreign companies in southern Iraq’s Basra.

news.headline: Real Madrid beat Celta Vigo to close on Barcelona with Valverde late goal
news.summary: Federico Valverde scores in injury-time as Real Madrid win 2-1 at Celta Vigo to maintain their La Liga title chase.

news.headline: Iranian UN ambassador condemns US-Israeli ‘criminal war’
news.summary: Iranian Ambassador to the United Nations Amir-Saeid Iravani urged the UN Security Council to stop the US-Israeli war.

news.headline: Athletic Bilbao vs Barcelona: La Liga – teams, start, lineups, kickoff time
news.summary: Barcelona were thumped in the Copa del Rey semifinal but return to La Liga on Saturday where they sit clear of Real.

news.headline: Mexico’s Sheinbaum pledges robust World Cup security in visit to Jalisco
news.summary: The Mexican president says 100,000 security personnel will be deployed during upcoming football tournament.

news.headline: Qatar partially reopens airspace as Iranian strikes continue to hit Gulf
news.summary: Evacuation and cargo flights to resume but scheduled services still suspended as US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

news.headline: Why did the US torpedo Iran’s ‘prize’ warship?
news.summary: Why did the US sink Iran's prize destroyer, the IRIS Dena? Soraya Lennie explains.

news.headline: People are tracking this shepherd fleeing Israeli strikes in Lebanon
news.summary: People are tracking a shepherd who has been walking with his herd for days across Lebanon, fleeing Israeli strikes.

news.headline: US issues limited licence for Venezuelan gold following high-level visit
news.summary: The licence follows a push from US President Donald Trump to open Venezuela's resource sector to outside investment.

news.headline: Strikes on Iran to last ‘four to six weeks’, White House says
news.summary: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt says the US expects the war on Iran to last "about four to six weeks”.

news.headline: US and Israel attacking Iran at ‘record pace’, war monitor says
news.summary: Airwars says first days of US-Israeli Iran strikes 'hit significantly more targets than any campaign in recent decades'.

news.headline: Eight years of captivity: Finding freedom and healing in Ukraine
news.summary: Four years after Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, many people are trying to overcome deep-rooted trauma and rebuild what has been lost.&nbsp;

news.headline: ‘The trauma will stay with me for life,’ says survivor of sexual violence in war-torn Ukraine
news.summary: One evening in war-ravaged southern Ukraine, a group of armed men entered Maryna’s home. She had nowhere to hide.

news.headline: Ukraine: ‘Use every diplomatic tool to end this war’, top UN official tells Security Council
news.summary: The international community must “use every diplomatic tool” to bring an end to the war in Ukraine, a senior UN official told the Security Council on Tuesday as Russia’s full-scale invasion entered a fifth year.

news.headline: LIVE: UN marks fourth anniversary of Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine
news.summary: Four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the UN is marking the day with high-level debate and renewed calls to end the war - including in the General Assembly which passed a resolution reaffirming its strong commitment to the sovereignty, independence and territorial inte

news.headline: Ukraine wakes to more violence as Russia’s full-scale invasion enters fifth year
news.summary: The full-scale invasion of Ukraine by Russian troops on 24 February 2022 shattered the peaceful aspirations of an entire continent, but war must never be the new normal, UN General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Myriad fragments, one tragedy: How four years of war changed Ukraine
news.summary: Although fighting had been raging in the east of Ukraine since Russia's invasion of Crimea in 2014, most in the country did not believe all-out war would occur. With the full-scale invasion now reaching the four-year mark, many Ukrainians cannot believe it has gone on for so long, with no end i

news.headline: Ukraine: $588 billion recovery cost over the next 10 years
news.summary: Reconstruction and recovery in Ukraine will cost almost $588 billion over the next decade, or nearly three times the estimated GDP in 2025, as housing, energy and other critical sectors continue to come under fire from Russian forces.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine’s women at breaking point after four years of war as attacks on energy, healthcare continue – UN humanitarians
news.summary: Four years into Russia’s full-scale invasion, millions in Ukraine struggle to keep the lights on and heat their homes, with the crisis taking a particular toll on women, humanitarians warned on Friday.

news.headline: Four years of war in Ukraine: Childhood has ‘moved underground’, displacement continues – UN humanitarians
news.summary: Civilian suffering shows no sign of letting up in Ukraine as the four-year-mark of Russia’s full-scale invasion nears amid attacks on energy infrastructure, blackouts and freezing temperatures, UN humanitarians warned on Tuesday.

news.headline: As conditions worsen in Ukraine, refugees struggle to return
news.summary: As Ukraine prepares to enter the fifth year of the full-scale Russian invasion on 24 February, UN monitors say harm to civilians has “demonstrably worsened”, while energy attacks and freezing temperatures are making it harder for displaced families to return.

news.headline: Ukraine: UN aid convoy reaches frontlines in Dnipro
news.summary: A UN humanitarian convoy reached frontline communities in Ukraine’s Dnipro region on Wednesday, delivering critical medical and hygiene supplies as fighting continues to take a heavy toll on civilians and infrastructure across the country.

news.headline: Measles cases drop in 2025 across Europe and Central Asia, but outbreak risks remain
news.summary: Measles cases across Europe and Central Asia declined by 75 per cent in 2025 compared to 2024, according to preliminary data released on Wednesday by the World Health Organization (WHO), which warned of remaining outbreak risks.

news.headline: General Assembly President urges Europeans to ‘stand up’ for the UN
news.summary: The President of the United Nations General Assembly appealed on Tuesday for Europe to protect the international rules-based system, defend the truth in the face of fake news and other falsehoods, and support UN reform.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine war keeps nuclear safety on a knife-edge, UN watchdog warns
news.summary: Attacks on Ukraine’s power system highlight how the ongoing war threatens the safety of the country’s nuclear facilities, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) warned on Friday.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine: Civilians injured, miners killed, in separate Russian attacks
news.summary: A fresh wave of Russian strikes overnight across Ukraine injured several people and left thousands “without heat in the heart of winter,” the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country said on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: From Cape Town to London, Deo Kato runs to challenge racism and reclaim the migration narrative
news.summary: Step by step, mile by mile, Deo Kato ran his way across a continent — and beyond. After a year and a half on the road, the Ugandan British runner and campaigner has become the first person to run from Cape Town to London.

news.headline: UN watchdog warns Ukraine war remains world’s biggest threat to nuclear safety
news.summary: The war in Ukraine remains the world’s biggest threat to nuclear safety as a fifth year of combat looms, the head of the UN nuclear watchdog warned on Friday, citing continued risks to power supplies at nuclear sites vulnerable to fighting nearby.

news.headline: ‘Cycle of attacks must end’: Lead UN official in Ukraine
news.summary: The senior UN official in Ukraine, Matthias Schmale, has issued a condemnation of the massive overnight Russian drone and missile strike on several major Ukrainian cities, killing and injuring civilians, and knocking out energy infrastructure amid sub-zero temperatures.

news.headline: Keeping people warm amid hostilities and harsh winter weather in Ukraine
news.summary: As people in war-torn Ukraine face the coldest winter in more than a decade, authorities and humanitarians are working to help them stay warm, particularly the most vulnerable residents.&nbsp;

news.headline: Cold and dark: UN rights chief condemns Russian strikes on Ukraine’s power grid
news.summary: Russian attacks on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure have cut heat, electricity and water to hundreds of thousands of civilians in freezing winter conditions, prompting the UN human rights chief to denounce the strikes as “cruel” and a clear violation of international law.
